response.justification: Bogdan is a capable full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship values and broad distributed systems exposure, but he does not meet the minimum qualifications for this role. The most critical gap is C++: the role requires 8 years of C++ programming experience, while his resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. C++ does not even appear in his skills list. Beyond that, his infrastructure experience is application-layer cloud usage (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployments) rather than the compute platform engineering the role demands, and there is no evidence of a formal technical leadership or mentoring role at the scale required.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship culture — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development applied consistently across multiple roles
Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency design)
Community leadership and knowledge-sharing via workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating some mentoring capacity
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI in 2014–2015 and a thesis project) versus the 8-year hard minimum requirement
No cloud compute platform engineering experience; cloud exposure is limited to application-level AWS CDK usage and Kubernetes cluster deployment
No demonstrated formal technical leadership role mentoring engineers on infrastructure or cloud systems at meaningful scale

key_factors.factor: Software craftsmanship and distributed systems exposure
